curation.synopsis: The Lost trilogy's second movement opens with the Gereon 12 extracted back to friendly space after eighteen months behind the lines, only to find that their long absence has reshaped the regiment. Major Elim Rawne has been confirmed in command of the Tanith First; Gaunt is demoted from Colonel-Commissar to Commissar, his Inquisitorial-supervised return treated as a political problem rather than a triumphant homecoming. The action moves to Ancreon Sextus, where a new third-intake of Belladon recruits is being folded into the regiment alongside the surviving Tanith and Verghastite Ghosts under Hark and Daur. The Gereon survivors' Chaos taint is treated medically and politically; Gaunt's path back to authority runs through the local Sabbat-Crusade command staff and a quiet, painful adjustment of who-commands-whom that culminates in a battle where the Ghosts are forced into action against an Arch-Enemy push despite their internal divisions. By novel's end Gaunt has clawed back nominal command authority, but the regiment is permanently transformed.
